steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers conclude that landscape variations affect net CO₂ in ways most models are too coarse to capture. To support this, a finding must show that finer-resolution models (1 km²) and coarser-resolution models (10 km²) produce different results for the same metric — proving that landscape detail is lost at coarser scales.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: describes a seasonal pattern shared across both resolutions — shows agreement, not a resolution-dependent difference. ✗
  • Choice B: reports that two variables are independent of each other — says nothing about what coarse models miss. ✗
  • Choice C — different heterotrophic respiration rates in autumn between the 1 km² and 10 km² models: directly shows that the two resolutions produce divergent results for the same metric, proving coarser grids lose landscape information. ✓
  • Choice D: shows the models reached identical conclusions despite using different data — the opposite of what is needed to support the claim. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
